criteria.comment: All 3 input items appear in chat_records with no silently dropped items. items_dropped is empty and every source_id from the train fixture slice is represented in the output.

Deploy thread correctly marked 'high', SurrealDB progress thread correctly marked 'high'. PR review request correctly marked 'mid'. Matches corpus_meta expected_signal_levels exactly. Minor deduction: the model did not surface the 'curated' tier that appears in linear/linear tickets, but that source type is not present in this Slack slice.

Alice Chen and Bob Kim both appear as entities_mentioned with correct roles (approver, reviewer). #deployments and #ai-engineering channels are captured. #code-review channel is captured. Aliases (@alice, @bob) are not stored as separate stubs here — they appear inline in content, which is acceptable for triage phase; full alias resolution happens in Phase 3b entity_resolver.

All summaries are ≤3 sentences and accurate. The deploy summary correctly captures action + approver + outcome. The HNSW summary correctly captures the technical decision. The PR review summary is slightly thin (omits the PR number context) but captures the intent. Overall concise and faithful.

All required fields are present: external_id, title, summary, content, signal_level, source_type, occurred_at, entities_mentioned. integration_metadata block is fully populated. TriagePyd Pydantic model would validate cleanly against this output.

overall_comment: Strong triage output for the Slack integration. Signal level assignment is accurate, entity recall is good, and all items are represented. The main improvement area is summary_quality — the PR review summary could be more specific about the artifact (PR #5, microbots enrichment layer) rather than just the action. json_schema_validity would improve further if entity aliases were included as stubs rather than left implicit in content strings. Overall this is a passing baseline (4.05 / 5.0 > threshold 3.0).

_meta.note: Hand-scored baseline using rubric weights. Replace with live judge run: uv run python tests/eval/judge.py --phase triage --input tests/eval/judge_input_triage.json
